title: else statements should be clearly matched with an if
description: "else" statements should be clearly matched with an "if"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe danglingelseproblem appears when nestedif/elsestatements are written without curly braces. In this case,elseis associated with the nearestifbut that is not always obvious and sometimes the indentation can also be misleading.This rules reportselsestatements that are difficult to understand, because they are inside nestedifstatements without curly braces.Adding curly braces can generally make the code clearer (see ruleS121), and in this situation of danglingelse, it really clarifies the intention of the code.Noncompliant code exampleif (a) if (b) d++; else // Noncompliant, is the "else" associated with "if(a)" or "if (b)"? (the answe
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Dangling else detected - use braces to clarify which if the else belongs to.

nested_if_without_braces.description: Nested if statements without braces followed by else
